candidates.name: p,p'-DDT
candidates.promotion: High promotion potential — parent of confirmed positive DDE with a direct amyloid mechanism; curate CTD-AD.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-AD DirectEvidence; assay AβPP/BACE1 induction to confirm the amyloidogenic MIE.
candidates.rationale: Organochlorine; parent compound of the confirmed AD positive DDE. Increases AβPP and BACE1 (amyloidogenic) and is associated with cognitive decline.

evidence.detail: Increases AβPP and BACE1 in vitro (amyloidogenic); parent of confirmed positive DDE.

candidates.name: dieldrin
candidates.promotion: Very close — a known neurotoxicant already curated for PD; check CTD-AD DirectEvidence for a cross-disease promotion.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-AD DirectEvidence (cross-disease promotion from the PD set).
candidates.rationale: Organochlorine insecticide; a confirmed Parkinson's positive, with a quantified AD association.

evidence.detail: Single case-control OR 2.09 (1.22–3.56) for AD (the Yan 2016 pooled OR was 1.34).
evidence.source: Singh et al., Hum Exp Toxicol 2013 (via Yan et al. 2016)

candidates.name: chlorpyrifos
candidates.promotion: Cross-disease positive whose AChE mechanism maps onto AD cholinergic AOPs; curate CTD-AD.
candidates.rationale: Organophosphate; a confirmed Parkinson's positive. AChE inhibition maps onto AD cholinergic pathways and it is named in AD reviews.

candidates.name: arsenic (inorganic)
candidates.promotion: High promotion potential — deep mechanistic AD literature; resolve the arsenite salt form and curate CTD-AD.
candidates.experiment: Resolve the correct arsenite DTXSID (salt-form rule), then curate CTD-AD DirectEvidence.
candidates.rationale: Metalloid with an extensive mechanistic AD literature (amyloid, tau, oxidative stress).

evidence.detail: Multiple mechanistic AD studies (amyloid, tau, oxidative stress).
evidence.source: Bakulski et al., J Alzheimers Dis 2020
Bakulski et al., J Alzheimers Dis 2020

candidates.name: beta-hexachlorocyclohexane
candidates.promotion: Strong association reproduced across two studies; needs a mechanistic MIE to clear.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-AD; seek an organochlorine mechanistic MIE.
candidates.rationale: Organochlorine (β-HCH); a quantified AD association reproduced across a meta-analysis and an ExWAS.

evidence.detail: Single case-control OR 2.06 (1.04–3.10) for AD; also NHANES ExWAS cognition.
evidence.source: Singh et al., Hum Exp Toxicol 2013 (via Yan 2016); Jang et al., Exposome 2025

candidates.name: heptachlor epoxide
candidates.promotion: Association-only; organochlorine class-consistent; curate for mechanism.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-AD; watch for mechanistic support.
candidates.rationale: Organochlorine metabolite; associated with cognitive decline in an ExWAS.

evidence.detail: NHANES ExWAS: associated with cognitive decline.

candidates.name: thallium
candidates.promotion: Weak single-study association — watch-list, not a wet-lab priority yet.
candidates.rationale: Heavy metal; a single-study ExWAS association with worse cognition.

evidence.detail: NHANES ExWAS: associated with worse cognition (single study).

candidates.name: glyphosate
candidates.promotion: Emerging — animal + mechanistic only, human epidemiology thin; held in the watch tier.
candidates.experiment: Await human epidemiology; a targeted AD-model / iPSC neuroinflammation assay would clarify.
candidates.rationale: Herbicide (and its AMPA metabolite) persists in brain and exacerbates AD-like pathology and neuroinflammation in mice after a 6-month washout; human causal evidence is still thin. The honest pre-empt for the "why no glyphosate?" question.

evidence.detail: Persists in brain; exacerbates AD-like pathology/neuroinflammation in mice after a 6-month washout (animal, not human).

candidates.name: curcumin
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (dietary polyphenol) — flagged by activity models, but no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: donepezil
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (anti-amyloid/cholinergic drug) — no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: epigallocatechin gallate
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (dietary polyphenol) — no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: methylene blue
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (anti-tau candidate) — no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: galantamine
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (cholinergic drug) — no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: resveratrol
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: AD-assay-active decoy (dietary polyphenol) — no curated causal AD signal. Must rank last.

backtest.method: DDE's curated CTD-AD DirectEvidence was withheld; it was scored on its convergent non-curated strands alone, then compared to the best adversarial-decoy score.

evidence.detail: Elevated serum DDE in Alzheimer's patients.
